paper_metadata.title: The public’s views on climate policies in seven large global south countries
paper_metadata.authors: Richard T. Carson; Jiajun Lu; Emily A. Khossravi; Gunnar Köhlin; Erik Sterner; Thomas Sterner; Dale Whittington
paper_metadata.geographic_scope: Chile, Colombia, India, Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, Vietnam
paper_metadata.empirical_scope_or_sample: YouGov online survey, n=8,400 adults (about 1,200 per country) collected April–May 2023 (internet-enabled samples).
paper_metadata.carbon_tax_relevance_summary: Best–worst preferences over earmarks for carbon tax revenue (health/education, solar and clean R&D, targeted vs uniform rebates, roads, and deficit reduction).

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Earmark carbon tax revenue to education and health
determinants.definition: Using carbon tax revenue to fund education and health programmes (instead of returning it equally to all citizens).
determinants.mechanism: Earmarking to highly valued public services likely increases perceived fairness and visible benefits, raising carbon tax revenue acceptability.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; options compared to 'Return in equal shares' as the reference.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Spend on education and health'—Combined rank 1; coefficient 1.195*** vs 'Return in equal shares'.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Combined rank=1; coefficient vs equal-shares ref=1.195***.
determinants.notes: Highest in all countries except Kenya (rank 2).

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Earmark carbon tax revenue to clean R&D
determinants.definition: Invest carbon tax revenue in clean energy research and development to reduce future technology costs.
determinants.mechanism: Positions the tax as funding solutions and innovation for future decarbonization, supporting perceived effectiveness and legitimacy.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; relative to 'Return in equal shares' reference.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Invest in clean R&D'—Combined rank 3; coefficient 0.916*** vs 'Return in equal shares'.
determinants.notes: Consistently among the top revenue-use options.

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Earmark carbon tax revenue to solar panel subsidies
determinants.definition: Use carbon tax revenue to subsidize solar panel installations.
determinants.mechanism: Provides tangible renewable-energy support, likely increasing perceived usefulness and immediate benefits from the carbon tax.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; relative to 'Return in equal shares' reference.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Subsidize solar panels'—Combined rank 2; coefficient 0.956*** vs 'Return in equal shares'.

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Targeted rebates of carbon tax revenue to the poor
determinants.definition: Return carbon tax revenue specifically to poor households (targeted compensation).
determinants.mechanism: Addresses distributional concerns by compensating those seen as more vulnerable, increasing perceived fairness of the tax.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; compared to 'Return in equal shares' reference.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Return to poor'—Combined rank 5; coefficient 0.276*** vs 'Return in equal shares'.
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Combined rank=5; coefficient=0.276*** (Vietnam coefficient 0.054, no star).
determinants.notes: More preferred than uniform equal-share rebates on average.

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Uniform equal-share dividend of carbon tax revenue
determinants.definition: Return carbon tax revenue equally to all citizens (uniform rebates/dividend).
determinants.mechanism: Less targeted compensation may be perceived as less fair or less aligned with priority uses, reducing preference relative to earmarking.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; treated as the base reference option.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Return in equal shares' is the base reference; Combined rank 7 (lowest).
determinants.notes: Lowest-ranked overall use of carbon tax revenue.

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Use carbon tax revenue to reduce the government deficit
determinants.definition: Apply carbon tax revenue to reduce the government budget deficit.
determinants.mechanism: Often seen as less directly beneficial (lower perceived value) but may be viewed more positively where fiscal/financial priorities dominate.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; compared to 'Return in equal shares' reference across countries.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Reduced government deficit'—Combined rank 6; coefficient 0.027 (no overall significance marker).
determinants.statistical_or_result_detail: Combined rank=6; coefficient=0.027; Kenya rank 5 (0.609***), Chile rank 7 (−0.241***).
determinants.notes: Country heterogeneity: negative in Chile/South Africa/Vietnam; positive in Kenya/Nigeria.

determinants.standardized_candidate_label: Earmark carbon tax revenue to road investment
determinants.definition: Invest carbon tax revenue in roads.
determinants.mechanism: A visible earmark for local infrastructure may increase perceived legitimacy versus cash or deficit uses, though it is not the top-ranked option.
determinants.exact_tax_policy_context: Best–worst ranking of uses for carbon tax revenue; compared to 'Return in equal shares' reference.
determinants.outcome_variable: Ranked preference (1=most preferred) for carbon tax revenue earmarks.
determinants.evidence_quote: Table 6: 'Invest in roads'—Combined rank 4; coefficient 0.542*** vs 'Return in equal shares'.
determinants.notes: Ranked above equal-share dividends across countries.

exclusions_or_uncertainties.issue: Missing respondent-level determinants for carbon tax acceptability
exclusions_or_uncertainties.reason: The provided carbon-tax-specific results (Table 6) report average ranking of earmark options by country without regression covariates linking demographics/ideology/knowledge to carbon-tax revenue acceptance.
